steps.title: Identify the starting value
steps.content: When $x=0$, the value of $y$ is $80$. In an exponential model of the form $y=a(b)^x$, this means $a=80$.

steps.title: Convert the percent increase
steps.content:
Increasing by $15\%$ means each new value is $115\%$ of the previous value:
$$
1+0.15=1.15
$$
So the growth factor is $1.15$.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content: Using starting value $80$ and growth factor $1.15$, the model is $$ y=80(1.15)^x $$ The answer is A.
